title: [FIRRTL][IMCP]  Should not be propagating invalid values through wires/regs/module ports
body:
This is another attempt to find some bad behaviour in IMCP

```scala
circuit Foo :
  module Foo :
    input clock: Clock
    input reset: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<8>

    inst bar of Bar

    reg r : UInt<8>, clock with:
      (reset => (reset, UInt<8>(4)))
    r <= bar.b
    b <= r

  module Bar :
    output b: UInt<8>
    b is invalid
```
MFC:
```verilog
module Foo(	// ./x.fir:2:10
  input        clock, reset,
  output [7:0] b);

  Bar bar ();	// ./x.fir:7:5
  assign b = 8'h4;	// ./x.fir:2:10, :9:5
endmodule

module Bar();	// ./x.fir:14:10
endmodule
```
SFC:
```verilog
module Foo(
  input        clock,
  input        reset,
  output [7:0] b
);
  reg [7:0] r;
  assign b = r;
  always @(posedge clock) begin
    if (reset) begin
      r <= 8'h4;
    end else begin
      r <= 8'h0;
    end
  end
endmodule
```
